prevalence.source: Ministério da Saúde/PNDS + pediatric nutrition syntheses
prevalence.trend: slowly decreasing with supplementation programs; persists in vulnerable groups

burden.mortalityRate: Low; indirect via infection risk and development

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Low hemoglobin with microcytosis/hypochromia; ferritin (interpret with inflammation), transferrin saturation; evaluate diet and blood loss

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; screening during child health visits is not universal
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with supplementation and adherence

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: CBC available; ferritin testing variable; nutrition assessment in APS
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Oral iron supplementation, dietary counseling, treat parasitosis when indicated; follow-up hemoglobin response
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Adherence to supplementation and food insecurity in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Primary care child health programs and low-cost iron supplementation
